Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Two studies in compiler optimisations 113 upvotes, 15 comments
  2. Personal Encyclopedias 556 upvotes, 115 comments
  3. Obsolete Sounds 133 upvotes, 22 comments
  4. More precise elevation data for GraphHopper routing engine 67 upvotes, 10 comments
  5. The EU still wants to scan your private messages and photos 1338 upvotes, 363 comments
  6. European Parliament decided that Chat Control 1.0 must stop 400 upvotes, 104 comments
  7. Optimization lessons from a Minecraft structure locator 33 upvotes, 4 comments
  8. My home network observes bedtime with OpenBSD and pf 21 upvotes, 2 comments
  9. From zero to a RAG system: successes and failures 170 upvotes, 46 comments
  10. Running Tesla Model 3's computer on my desk using parts from crashed cars 746 upvotes, 250 comments
  11. My DIY FPGA board can run Quake II 199 upvotes, 58 comments
  12. End of "Chat Control": EU Parliament Stops Mass Surveillance in Voting Thriller 208 upvotes, 63 comments
  13. Cory Doctorow: Interoperability Can Save the Open Web 20 upvotes, 3 comments
  14. Government agencies buy commercial data about Americans in bulk 174 upvotes, 63 comments
  15. Intel Announces Arc Pro B70 and Arc Pro B65 GPUs 12 upvotes, 1 comments
  16. Shell Tricks That Make Life Easier (and Save Your Sanity) 307 upvotes, 132 comments
  17. Moving from GitHub to Codeberg, for lazy people 149 upvotes, 60 comments
  18. Meta and YouTube Found Negligent in Social-Media Addiction Trial 23 upvotes, 5 comments
  19. Earthquake scientists reveal how overplowing weakens soil at experimental farm 189 upvotes, 104 comments
  20. Swift 6.3 220 upvotes, 129 comments
  21. LibreOffice and the Art of Overreacting 140 upvotes, 78 comments
  22. Niche Museums 56 upvotes, 26 comments
  23. ARC-AGI-3 457 upvotes, 294 comments
  24. Asbestos, talc, and The Lancet's 1977 publication 14 upvotes, 4 comments
  25. Landmark L.A. jury verdict finds Instagram, YouTube were designed to addict kids 205 upvotes, 143 comments
  26. Marriage over, €100k down; AI users whose lives were wrecked by delusion 59 upvotes, 34 comments
  27. The truth that haunts the Ramones: 'They sold more T-shirts than records' 212 upvotes, 162 comments
  28. What came after the 486? 103 upvotes, 84 comments
  29. Newly purchased Vizio TVs now require Walmart accounts to use smart features 67 upvotes, 56 comments
  30. Ashby (YC W19) Is Hiring Engineers Who Make Product Decisions 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 11:30pm, 26 March 2026. Source here.
